tasks.name: Implement decomposition functions for all 7 record types
Implement schema drift logger for overflow fields
tasks.note: drift.rs was implemented and committed alongside decompose.rs in Task 1 because decompose.rs has a compile-time dependency on drift::log_overflow. All Task 2 verification criteria (4 unit tests + log_record_overflow convenience wrapper test) were satisfied in the same commit.

deviations.description: drift.rs was necessarily created alongside decompose.rs rather than in a separate Task 2 commit, because decompose.rs imports and calls drift::log_overflow for every record type decomposition. Splitting them into separate commits would have required either a stub/placeholder pattern or a non-compiling intermediate state. Both files were implemented fully in a single commit to maintain compile-and-test-at-every-commit discipline.
deviations.impact: none — all Task 2 functionality and tests are present and verified; the only difference is commit granularity

verification.cargo_test_store: pass — 18 tests (12 decompose + 5 drift + 1 db), 0 failures
verification.record_type_coverage: all 7 variants tested via dispatcher (user, assistant, progress, system, queue-operation, summary, file-history-snapshot)
verification.user_decomposition: sessions + messages + message_content rows verified for both string and block content forms
verification.assistant_decomposition: sessions + messages + message_content (text, tool_use, thinking) + tool_executions + token_usage rows verified
verification.progress_decomposition: progress_events row with correct data_type extraction verified
verification.system_decomposition: system_events row with subtype, duration_ms, extra_json verified
verification.file_history_snapshot: debug-logged without error, no target table in Phase 1
verification.idempotency: INSERT OR IGNORE prevents duplicate rows on re-decomposition, verified
verification.drift_logging: overflow fields logged to schema_drift_log with truncation and UNIQUE deduplication
verification.drift_qualified_types: assistant records log 3 qualified types: assistant, assistant.message, assistant.message.usage
